sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To ensure the Responsible Pharmacist (RP) role is performed compliantly and with clear handover, documentation, and operational control.

sections.heading: 2.1 Start of RP duty (on arrival)
steps: Confirm RP status and ensure only one RP is in place.
Complete the Responsible Pharmacist Notice legibly and display it prominently.
Update the RP Log with name, GPhC number, and start time.
Review handover/communications book, outstanding tasks, clinical issues, and confirm SOP suite is current.

sections.heading: 2.2 During RP duty
steps: Maintain safe supervision and delegation aligned to the Belfield role matrix (RPSOP 04).
Ensure secure handling of prescriptions and locked patient-identifiable documentation.
Ensure patient-facing services are delivered only when competent staff and required IT access are available.

sections.heading: 2.3 End of RP duty (leaving for the day)
steps: Record finish time in the RP Log.
Remove/amend the RP Notice.
Document key handover items: clinical queries, CD concerns, near misses, or service follow-ups.

metadata.applies_to: Pharmacist, Dispensing Assistants, Driver, MCA staff

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To define what activities may continue safely when the RP is temporarily absent, and what activities must stop.

sections.heading: 2.1 What can continue during RP absence
steps: Ordering and receiving stock excluding Controlled Drugs.
Putting stock away and date checking excluding Controlled Drugs.
Cleaning and routine housekeeping.
Accessing Titan PMR for admin tasks, filing, retrieving EPS, or printing tokens.
Receiving prescriptions and assembling administrative bundles.
Responding to general non-clinical enquiries.

sections.heading: 2.2 What must NOT occur during RP absence
steps: No dispensing and no labelling.
No sale/supply of P medicines.
No handing out dispensed prescriptions/medicines to patients/representatives.
No dispatch for delivery (no handing out delivery bags to driver).
No clinical checks or pharmacist-only decisions.

sections.heading: 2.4 Maximum absence rule
sections.content: The RP may be absent for up to 2 hours total in any 24-hour period. If exceeded, activities requiring the RP must stop.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To ensure delegation is safe, consistent, and auditable, and that staff competence is recorded.

sections.heading: 2. Procedure
steps: Review task lists and add Belfield-specific workflows.
Tick and authorize which tasks each role may perform based on training.
Maintain staff competency records containing qualifications and RP signatures.
Review triggers: every 2 years, when new tasks launch, or after incidents.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To define which roles can undertake which tasks at Belfield Pharmacy, and to support safe delegation and auditability.

sections.heading: 2. How to use
sections.content: This matrix is a local authorisation tool. A tick indicates 'may perform once trained and competent'. Pharmacist-only constraints must be respected.

sections.heading: 3. Task Matrix Template
sections.content: Insert local authorization layout here mapping roles against Titan PMR, PharmOutcomes, and delivery apps.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To ensure Belfield Pharmacy identifies and meets patients' communication support needs related to disability or sensory loss via Ask, Record, Flag, Share, Act, Review.

sections.heading: 2. Steps
steps: ASK: Enquire about communication needs at first registration.
RECORD: Input specific requirements clearly into Titan PMR.
FLAG: Make constraints highly visible using prefixes like 'AIS:'.
SHARE: Disclose preferences securely with other healthcare professionals.
ACT: Provide alternative formats like larger fonts, SMS, or emails.
REVIEW: Re-verify choices periodically during NMS or service touchpoints.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To ensure prescriptions are received, triaged, and booked-in safely, with correct patient details and charge handling.

sections.heading: 2. Steps
steps: Identify request style: EPS token or paper.
Establish if patient is waiting or returning.
Validate fields (name, address, age if under 12).
Confirm validity windows (6 months standard, 28 days for Schedule 2-4 CDs).
Record exemption state or process correct prescription charges.
Route task into the processing workflow layout queue in Titan.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To ensure each prescription is clinically appropriate, legally valid, and safe for supply at the time of dispensing.

sections.heading: 2. Appropriateness Checks
steps: Verify indication, dose, frequency, route, and formulation.
Cross-reference allergies, interactions, and duplication risks.
Audit high-risk profiles: narrow therapeutic index drugs, steroid tapers, weekly methotrexate, or paediatric doses.
Review Opioids: Check starting doses, safe titration, and co-prescribing sedation risks.
Log interventions inside Titan and write relevant counselling prompts.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To ensure dispensing labels are generated accurately from the prescription and Titan PMR, with appropriate checks for clarity, patient identity, warnings and endorsements.

sections.heading: 2. Protocol Rules
steps: Verify script clarity before running labels; scale queries up to the pharmacist.
Double-check calculations for variable doses or paediatrics.
Confirm Titan PMR matches exactly. Handle duplicate name warnings securely.
Review automated notes for brand selections, compliance aids, or AIS text flags.
Generate software labels without manual text changes.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To ensure items are selected, assembled and prepared safely, using the prescription as the primary source and preserving an audit trail for checking.

sections.heading: 2. Steps
steps: Use the physical prescription or token as primary source, not just the text label.
Pick components into basket and verify expiration markers.
Count quantities and keep bulk or empty containers inside the basket for accuracy checking evidence.
Write open dates and calculated expiries on bulk liquid medications.
Apply text labels cleanly without hiding critical tracking indices, markings, or braille.

sections.heading: 3. Owings Management
steps: Print an owing voucher via Titan if stock is low.
Remind CD patients that remaining balances must be collected within the initial 28-day script validity framework.
Audit outstanding owing queues twice daily.
Process incoming stock against original orders before sending to accuracy check.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
sections.content: To ensure the dispensed product and label exactly match the prescription and are safe to supply, with errors captured for learning.

sections.heading: 2. Core Checklist
steps: Confirm a pharmacist clinical check is present.
Enforce ACT boundaries: do not pass CDs, high-risk items, or complex interactions to an ACT.
Match label details, container choices, and final content counts against the script.
Log errors instantly inside the near-miss log.
Bag finalized lines; split fridge items into clear bags marked 'FRIDGE'.
Annotate items needing reconstitution so they are not supplied dry.

sections.heading: 1. Standard Hand-out
steps: Identify patient via name, address, and DOB.
Retrieve main items along with secondary locations (fridge or CD locker).
Verify labels match tracking numbers.
Take exemptions or payments.
Follow attached pharmacist counselling tags.
Use antibiotic counselling prompts during handover.

sections.heading: 2. High Risk items
steps: Verify CD scripts fall inside 28-day limits. Capture signatures.
Physically open and show insulin boxes to patients to confirm expected brands.
Do not hand out items during an RP absence unless a 'Checked & Bagged' DSOP 13 protocol is explicitly authorized.

sections.heading: 1. Workflow
steps: Explain the eRD mechanism to patients during setup.
At each cycle, verify items remain appropriate.
Ask: Are you still taking this? Any side effects? Any hospital adjustments?
Escalate problems to the pharmacist and log details on Titan.
Mark unused items as 'Not Dispensed' via the electronic claim message.
Warn patients on their final issue to request a new batch from their GP.

sections.heading: 1. Protocol
steps: Check for available licensed alternatives before placing orders.
Match received supplies against invoices and prescriptions.
Verify Certificate of Conformity details.
Quarantine discrepancies immediately.
Retain supply pathways, batch histories, and tracking indices for 5 years minimum.
Submit ADR reactions to the MHRA Yellow Card system.

sections.heading: 1. Rules
steps: Confirm a valid SSP is running in England.
Verify replacement strategies fall inside protocol constraints.
Secure patient consent.
Apply correct layout text labels stating supply under SSP plus the reference number.
Endorse electronic claims with specific SSP markers, invoice codes, and pack sizes.

sections.heading: 1. Validation
steps: Verify animal type, owner data, and prescriber indicators.
Ensure Schedule 2/3 veterinary scripts contain RCVS numbers.
Enforce 28-day maximum validity timelines on controlled vet items.
Print label stating: 'For animal treatment only - keep out of reach of children'.
Retain purchase logs, batch files, and prescriptions for 5 years minimum.

sections.heading: 1. Process
steps: Confirm signature, registration details, and date rules.
Cross-reference prescriber status via regulatory databases.
Log entries into the POM Register within 24 hours.
Retain private scripts and record books for 2 years minimum from the last entry date.
Route private Schedule 2/3 scripts directly to the NHSBSA via standardized submission pathways.

sections.heading: 1. Workflow
steps: Pull scripts via Titan EPS queues.
Check NHS Spine tracker if nominations are missing.
Change nominations only after obtaining explicit patient consent.
Audit quantity translation anomalies on the Spine interface layout.
Transmit accurate dispense notifications before claiming reimbursement items.

sops.title: Handing Out Checked & Bagged Medicines (Pharmacist Authorisation)

sections.heading: 1. Operational Parameters
sections.content: Aligned with the 7 January 2026 supervision framework. Permits authorised staff to hand over pre-checked items during brief RP absences without altering standard RP accountability structures.

sections.heading: 2. Preconditions
steps: An RP must remain appointed for the premises.
Items must be sealed and explicitly marked as checked & bagged.
Pharmacists must log written or verbal authorization updates inside the communications book or log sheet.

sections.heading: 3. Exclusions
steps: No Controlled Drugs.
No High-Risk items (methotrexate, lithium, valproate).
No newly prescribed lines requiring clinical counselling.
No cold chain fridge items.
No items where patients explicitly request an RP interface.

sections.heading: 1. Delivery Preparation
steps: Log delivery consent and preferences in Titan PMR.
Apply warning text stickers: FRIDGE, CD, or REFER TO PHARMACIST.
Export files to the delivery app containing item counts and specific transit notes.
Load fridge units and CDs immediately before vehicle departure.

sections.heading: 2. Drop-off Execution
steps: Verify recipient name and address details at the doorstep.
Capture an e-signature or photograph within the delivery app layout.
Do not leave lines unattended unless an explicit safe-place document is logged.
Return failed deliveries to the pharmacy. Return stock to shelves, fridge, or CD safe before closing.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose & Controls
sections.content: Ensures FP10MDA instalment tracking for methadone and buprenorphine is run in accordance with Turning Point Rochdale escalation rules.

sections.heading: 2. Execution Steps
steps: Validate instalment instructions and CD writing rules.
Confirm script stays within its 28-day window.
Input alert headers on Titan: 'OST FP10MDA - missed dose rules apply'.
Log quantities, signatures, and dates on the physical layout chart of the FP10MDA document at supply.
Write CD Register rows immediately after drug handover, never in advance.

sections.heading: 1. Pre-supervision Rules
steps: Confirm a dose is currently due.
Verify identity elements using standard lookups.
Evaluate intoxication risks (slurred speech, sedation, unsteady gait).
Withhold doses if intoxication is suspected; immediately notify the pharmacist and Turning Point.

sections.heading: 2. Ingestion Review
steps: Measure liquids via calibrated cylinders or syringes.
Observe the patient swallowing methadone or waiting for sublingual buprenorphine to fully dissolve.
Log administration instances directly on the back of the FP10MDA template chart.

sections.heading: 1. Turning Point Rules
sections.content: 3 consecutive missed picks → SUSPEND supply. 4 or more misses across 14 days → CONTACT Hub.

sections.heading: 2. Procedure
steps: Mark records as NOT SUPPLIED on the text grid.
Add alert flags to Titan PMR profiles.
If 3 consecutive days are missed, place the script into the CD safe and mark status as SUSPENDED.
Call Turning Point Rochdale Hub on 01706 377197 or 0300 555 0234.
Do not restart supply until a new prescription document is issued by the hub.
